Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:算額]][[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:算額]][[Category:qq]]

{'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:算額]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ * メハラノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6150005005148 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木原町', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ * メハラノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6150005005148 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木原町', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]]

{'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|WD=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]]

Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|WD=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|WD=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|WD=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|WD=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|WD=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|WD=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|WD=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|WD=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|WD=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|WD=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|WD=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|WD=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|WD=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|WD=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|WD=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|WD=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ * メハラノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6150005005148 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木原町', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|qid=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|qid=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|qid=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|qid=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|qid=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|qid=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|qid=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|qid=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|qid=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|qid=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|qid=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|qid=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|qid=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|qid=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|qid=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|qid=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|qid=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|qid=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|qid=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|qid=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|qid=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ * メハラノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6150005005148 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県橿原市木原町', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '奈良県橿原市太田市町', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11610730|ltq=Q11610730|qid=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} [[JA:耳成山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11610732}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Miminashi-jinja.jpg|270px|Honden]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Kihara-chō |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|53.3|N|135|48|22.4|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Miminashi-yama
Yamaguchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine''' (みみなしやまぐちじんじゃ) is a shrine on {{ill|lt=Mount Miminashi|Q11610730|qid=Q11610730|1=Mount Miminashi}} (one of the {{ill|lt=Yamato Sanzan|Q1470150|qid=Q1470150|1=Yamato Sanzan}}) in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Though called a {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Shrine|Q11467399|qid=Q11467399|1=Yamaguchi Shrine}}, it is not at the mountain foot but around the eight-tenths point up the mountain. It is one of the six Yamaguchi shrines of {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (Asuka, Ishimura, {{ill|lt=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine|Q11578590|qid=Q11578590|lt=Unebi|1=Unebiyamaguchi Shrine}}, Osa, Hase, and '''Miminashi''') and was worshiped as the mountain deity of timber-cutting mountains for {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|qid=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} buildings. Also in the precinct are {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|qid=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Hakuryū Daijin|Q123407570|qid=Q123407570|1=Hakuryū Daijin}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth. === History === Its founding is unclearThere is a theory that it was founded by an oracle in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}., but old records state that in {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|qid=Q23563|1=730}} (Tenpyō 2) in the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|qid=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}} {{ill|lt=tax register|Q11545592|qid=Q11545592|1=tax register}}, the shrine received rice from the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, and that in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|qid=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and charged with {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} rites. Records also remain that in {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}} (Daidō 1) and in {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}} (Jōgan 1), imperial envoys conducted {{ill|lt=rain-prayer|Q477738|qid=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} rituals. In {{ill|lt=1651|Q6956|qid=Q6956|1=1651}} (Keian 4), villagers of Yamanobō cut and damaged sacred trees, triggering disputes over mountain management and ownership centered on the shrine. In {{ill|lt=1702|Q6819|qid=Q6819|1=1702}} (Genroku 15), it was settled by identifying “Miminashiyama Tenjin” as the shikinaisha Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine. After that, Yamanobō villagers transferred the divine spirit to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamanobō Yamaguchi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}''Great Encyclopedia of Temples and Shrines (Yamato/Kii)'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}). During this period, buildings were burned and fell into disrepair, but in August {{ill|lt=1748|Q7600|qid=Q7600|1=1748}} (Kan'en 1), parishioners of {{ill|lt=Miminashi Village|Q17175746|qid=Q17175746|1=Miminashi}} rebuilt the current {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} and {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, and the shrine came to be worshiped as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Ishiharada, Kihara, Kuzumoto, Shinga, Tokiwa, and Yamanobō districts. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (also read Takamimusubi-no-Kami) * {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} === Other === [[File:Miminashi-sangaku.jpg|thumb|A sangaku dedication]] *As a remnant of it once being called “Miminashi-yama,” the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} at the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|qid=Q667783|1=Sandō}} entrance is inscribed “Miminashi-yama.” *There is a {{ill|lt=sangaku|Q2569069|qid=Q2569069|1=Sangaku}} dedicated in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|qid=Q7670|1=1854}} (Ansei 1) bearing a {{ill|lt=wasan|Q1735738|qid=Q1735738|1=Japanese mathematics}} problem and solution. === Notes === === References === *On-site guide board *Ono Emio and Shimaoka Hiroshi, ''Photo Collection: Meiji, Taishō, Shōwa Kashihara: Memories of Hometown 65'', section on safe-childbirth amulet (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine), {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|qid=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 1979. === External links === * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_kankou/kankou/spot/miminashiyamaguchi.html Kashihara City - Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine] * [http://www.wasan.earth.linkclub.com/nara/miminasi.html Wasa-no-Yakata site (Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Miminashi Yamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:Sangaku]]
